samples.text: It is raining everywhere and raining nowhere at the same time. Is this logically consistent?
It rains in all places and in no places simultaneously. Does this make logical sense?
Rain falls everywhere and nowhere at the same moment. Is this logically consistent?
Everywhere and nowhere are complete opposites. Both cannot be true at once. Is the described situation logically consistent?
It is raining everywhere and raining nowhere at the same time. The meeting started at noon. Is this logically consistent?
